question: Which query-major local window is large enough to reduce repeated compressed-group dKV contributions before global memory without moving contribution production to group-major ownership?

first_runtime_gate.reason: This preserves the previously retained 32-token producer window and reaches the known 23.81x target write-reduction envelope. It is the safest comparable gate after the selected-tile partial-store rejection.

second_runtime_gate.reason: This reaches a similar 24.47x reduction with the same 128 tile count, but doubles the token window and may increase q/dO lifetime pressure.

aspirational_runtime_gate.reason: This models 47.25x write reduction, but each tile carries about 32K route pairs and is too wide for a first bring-up unless the reducer is split across waves/CTAs.

reject: same-slot/per-topk-column accumulation: only 1.03-1.07x reduction at useful windows
selected_block=64 local reducers: only 3.43x at token_window=32
global partial slabs: measured K128/K256 selected-tile compact partial-store loses to sparse atomics
decision.implementation_contract: Keep the query-major contribution producer and introduce full local segmented reduction across the token_window x selected_block route window before global memory. The runtime gate should compare against the 8.01 ms dot_k_atomic sparse-atomic wall and include full producer plus reducer time, not reducer-only timing.
decision.remaining_risk: The useful windows have p50 about 516-520 unique compressed groups and p99 near 1013-1019, so a dense active-group table or per-contribution LDS store will repeat the known 18 ms/133 ms failures. The implementation needs lane/warp/block segmented accumulation or a split-wave compact reducer rather than another active-slot table.
